recommendation: Surface as decision point — Remedies A/B/C may not fix this
reason: In-sample residuals are ALSO systematically positive. Over-prediction is not a regime-shift problem — it's a model misspecification problem. Need to investigate saturation curve shape, lift-prior pressure, or baseline allocation before choosing a remedy. None of A/B/C directly address this.

check_1_yoy.interpretation: If 2026/2025 < 0.90 → real YoY level shift (model would over-shoot on this alone). If 0.95–1.05 → no YoY drop; over-prediction comes from elsewhere (likely training-tail anomaly).

check_2_training_tail.interpretation: If ratio > 1.15 → Q4 2025 is elevated; model anchored on recent peak → over-shoots 2026. If 0.95–1.10 → no training-tail anomaly; over-prediction is structural (saturation/trend).

check_3_residuals.interpretation: If in-sample signed_mean ~0% AND OOT signed_mean > +10% → regime-shift (NOT a fit problem). The model fits training fine but the world changed in 2026. If in-sample signed_mean is ALSO systematically positive (>+5%) → the model is biased even within training; different root cause (model misspecification, not regime shift).
